title: thymus serpyllum subsp. lanuginosus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. lanuginosus, also known as Woolly Thyme or Woolly Wild Thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus serpyllum plant, a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its antimicrobial and antifungal effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of thymol and carvacrol compounds. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum subsp. lanuginosus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and herbal remedies. Thymus serpyllum subsp. lanuginosus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
